title: Mon May 4 - Carousel: What 3 AI Agents in 30 Days Actually Looks Like
content_text:
Most "AI agents" being sold to small businesses right now are just Zapier workflows with a ChatGPT wrapper.

Here's what an actual build looks like in 30 days.

Slide 1: Hook
Most "AI agents" being sold to SMBs are just Zapier workflows with a ChatGPT wrapper.
Here's what an actual build looks like in 30 days.

Slide 2: Week 1
Map every process in the business. Find the one that costs the most time for the least creativity.

Slide 3: Week 2
Build Agent #1. Not fancy. Just functional. It handles ONE thing reliably.

Slide 4: Week 3
Test it with real data. Real scenarios. Fix what breaks.

Slide 5: Week 4
Agent #1 is running. Your team actually uses it. You start Agent #2.

Slide 6: The Lesson
30 days gets you ONE working agent and proof it works.
Not a complete AI transformation. Not 15 automations. One thing. Running. Now.

Slide 7:
The businesses that actually transform do this 6 times in year one.
Sequentially. Building on what works.

Slide 8: The Outcome
The goal isn't the agent.
It's the evidence - that automation actually works in your business - so the next build gets approved faster.

Slide 9: CTA
What's the first process you'd automate if it only took 30 days?

#AIAgents #SmallBusiness #AIAutomation #PerthBusiness #BusinessEfficiency
notes: Carousel format. Dark background, white text, amber accent. Max 40 words per slide.

title: Tue May 5 - Text+Image: The Wrong First Automation
content_text:
Most SMB owners get sold the wrong first automation.

They hear "automate your emails" or "set up AI chatbots" and spend money on things that sound impressive but don't move the needle.

The first process you should automate is always the one that:
- Eats the most time
- Requires zero creativity
- Keeps happening whether you have capacity or not

For tradies: job quoting with custom materials and labour.
For accountants: data entry and reconciliation.
For dentists: appointment reminders and intake forms.

It is never the thing that looks sexiest in the demo.

The business that automates the boring stuff first is the one that has time to actually grow.

What's the most time-consuming boring task in your business right now?

#SmallBusiness #AIAutomation #SMB #BusinessEfficiency #Tradies
notes: Hook challenges the "sexy automation" narrative. Industry examples. Ends with question to drive comments.

title: Thu May 7 - Text+Image: Why I Stopped Offering AI Strategy
content_text:
I used to charge for AI strategy.

PowerPoint decks. Roadmaps. "Here's where AI can transform your business."

Clients loved the presentations. Most never implemented anything.

Why? Because a strategy document doesn't actually change how a business runs.

So I stopped selling strategy and started charging to build things.

The first thing I built for a client was a quoting tool that cut their job-quote time from 4 hours to 20 minutes.

They didn't need a roadmap to AI transformation.
They needed one thing fixed.

If you're selling AI to SMBs and you're leading with strategy, you're selling the wrong thing.

If you're an SMB owner and someone hands you a strategy document before they've built you anything, walk away.

#FounderJourney #AI #SmallBusiness #Consulting #BuildInPublic
notes: Personal founder post. Admits selling strategy was a mistake. Swap the 4 hours to 20 minutes stat for a real client result if available.
